contract_scope: Server-side persisted transcript for a chat session. Closes readiness tracker bullet V.8 (prompt/cache/checkpoint persistence). Consumed by GET /api/conversations/{session_id}/messages (#C-batch) and written by the inference HTTP + WS paths after each successful turn.
honesty_rules: session_id is client-supplied and NOT authenticated; any caller with the string can read or delete the corresponding messages. Matches the honesty rule on inference-request.contract.json. A future leaf may bind session_id to an authenticated identity.
seq is per-session and monotonically increasing starting at 0. Gaps are not expected; a non-monotonic seq indicates a bug in the append path.
request_id is populated only on assistant-role rows (the row written alongside the streaming response). system/user rows carry null request_id.

content is stored verbatim from the validated inference-request envelope (for user/system turns) or from the concatenated streamed delta text (for assistant turns). No truncation is applied at persist time.

This store is SQLite-only at the current leaf. Object-store-backed durable replay is a future extension — V.8 is ticked by this leaf because prompt+completion persistence is the core requirement; checkpoint/cache durability beyond SQLite is a separate hardening axis.
When the client re-sends the full messages[] on every turn (the #T-1 chat UI does this), the append path skips the first N entries that are already persisted to avoid duplicates.

rejection_codes.method_not_allowed: non-GET on /messages, non-GET/DELETE on session meta
